17.title: Introduction & Executive Summary - Subject Property Description

fields.description: Property address and location details
fields.extractedText: The subject property, located at 1101, 1121 109 St, North Battleford, SK, is a multi-family, walkup property with approximately 10,204 square feet of net rentable area.

fields.description: Building count, NRA, year built, occupancy, units, and style
fields.extractedText: The improvements are comprised of 2 total buildings, and consist of 10,204 square feet of net rentable area (NRA) as of the valuation date. The property, reportedly built in 1970; (1970 weighted) is approximately 100.0% occupied and features 16 units in a 2-story, garden style format.

fields.description: Purpose of the appraisal assignment
fields.extractedText: The purpose of this assignment is to provide the As Stabilized which at the time of inspection represents the existing improvements assuming stabilized occupancy as of the effective date for the property located at 1101, 1121 109 St, North Battleford, SK (herein referred to as the Subject property).

fields.description: Hypothetical conditions and assumptions related to market rents and stabilized occupancy
fields.note: Lengthy assumption block regarding as-if-stabilized valuation and market rent achievement

18.title: Introduction & Executive Summary - Ownership & Exposure Time

fields.description: Current owner of the subject property
fields.extractedText: The subject property is currently under the ownership of 102109845 Saskatchewan Ltd.

fields.description: Ownership change history and pending transactions
fields.extractedText: Ownership of the subject property has not changed in the past three years. We are unaware of any pending sales or listing activity relating to the subject property.

fields.description: Definition and explanation of exposure time concept
Distinction between marketing time and exposure time
Assumptions underlying the exposure time estimate
Concluded reasonable exposure and marketing time
fields.extractedText: Noting the subject property's physical, legal, economic and market characteristics, which are described further in this report, we have concluded a reasonable estimate of exposure and marketing time for the subject property to be six months.

19.title: Introduction & Executive Summary - Definitions & Value Scenarios

fields.description: CUSPAP definition of market value
fields.note: Standard market value definition in italics

fields.description: Key characteristics of market value (cash, unaffected by financing, competitive market, fair sale)
Definition of fee simple estate interest
Explanation of fee simple ownership and residential tenancy implications
Value scenarios included in appraisal
fields.note: Market analysis, Highest and Best Use analysis included

20.title: Introduction & Executive Summary - Assistance & Sources of Information

fields.description: Professional assistance provided by Paul Liboiron (Insight Home Inspections)
fields.extractedText: Paul Liboiron of Insight Home Inspections Ltd., a member of Alberta Professional Home Inspectors Society provided real property appraisal assistance to the appraisers signing this certification. Assistance provided included property inspection. Paul Liboiron is registered as a non-member with the AIC to provide professional assistance with real property inspection.

fields.description: Table of information sources (SAMA, Client, CoStar, ICS, etc.)
sources: Property Assessment & Tax - City of North Battleford Zoning
Building Size - CoStar | Confirmed by Local Agents

subsections: executive-summary-property-description (page 17): Property location, improvements, appraisal purpose, hypothetical conditions
executive-summary-ownership-exposure (page 18): Owner details, ownership history, exposure time analysis
executive-summary-definitions (page 19): Market value definition, property rights, value scenarios
executive-summary-assistance-sources (page 20): Professional assistance, sources of information table

mapping_notes: Pages 11-13: Photo grids showing interior, exterior, and system room photos (6 per page or single photo)
Pages 14-16: Three maps showing street-level, local area, and regional location context
Pages 17-20: Introduction & Executive Summary narrative text covering property description, ownership, valuation purpose, market value definition, and information sources
All pages follow consistent footer format with page number, property address, and file number
Hypothetical conditions section on Page 17 contains important assumption text regarding as-stabilized valuation
Sources of Information table on Page 20 lists 10 data source categories and their providers
Field IDs follow pattern: intro-photo-N for photos, intro-map-TYPE for maps, descriptive IDs for text/definition fields
